evals.prompt: I just created the fabric-apps monorepo with fabric-app-campus-profile as the first app. I need a root-level .gitignore that covers Node apps, Vite builds, environment variables, and Rayfin configs. What should I include?
evals.expected_output: A complete .gitignore file with sections for Node/npm, build artifacts (dist, .vite), environment files (.env*, rayfin/.env*), Rayfin deployment files, IDE/OS artifacts, and comments explaining each section.

evals.prompt: User describes a fix: 'I fixed the VegaVisual height override issue in the print pipeline by updating the columnMetadata format string instead of the spec.' What should the commit message be?
evals.expected_output: Formatted commit message: 'fix(campus-profile): override VegaVisual format via columnMetadata instead of spec' with optional body explaining why this approach prevents silent failures.

evals.prompt: I'm working on an ed-fi-pipelines repo (PySpark, Spark SQL, T-SQL). I need a PR template that enforces data scope tracking for FERPA compliance. Create it.
evals.expected_output: PR template with title format including domain tags, body sections for Description/Changes/Testing/Data Scope, explicit FERPA audit trail field, and examples using ed-fi contexts.

evals.prompt: How should I name my feature branches in the fabric-apps monorepo? What's the convention?
evals.expected_output: Branch naming pattern: 'feat/scope/description' with examples like 'feat/campus-profile/print-layout-fix', 'feat/infra/ci-workflow', plus brief branch strategy doc explaining protected branches (main=prod, staging=pre-prod).

evals.prompt: I need to set up GitHub Actions for the fabric-apps monorepo. It's a Node/TypeScript + Vite app with Vitest tests. Should run checks on every PR, deploy to staging on staging branch, and to production on main merge.
evals.expected_output: Three GitHub workflow files (.github/workflows/): test.yml (lint+build+test on PR), deploy-staging.yml (triggered by push to staging), deploy-prod.yml (triggered by push to main). Include env setup, conditional runs, and artifact handling.

evals.prompt: Review my PR before I merge it. I touched: src/queries/campus/digital-resource.dax, src/components/digital-resources-section.tsx, and src/print-compact.css in the campus-profile app. What should I check?
evals.expected_output: Context-aware code review checklist tailored to the campus-profile app: Correctness (DAX syntax, React/TypeScript), Readability, Architecture (follows Direct Lake pattern, respects rate-limiting), Security (no credential leaks), Data Sensitivity (FERPA-safe aggregation), and Print CSS mechanics (fixed Vega dims, constrainChartsForPrint usage).

evals.prompt: I want to add a second app (fabric-app-staffing-profile) to the fabric-apps monorepo. What's the setup checklist?
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step guide: Create folder structure (fabric-app-staffing-profile/src, package.json, etc.), add .claude/CLAUDE.md (project instructions), update root .gitignore if needed, add app to root CI workflow (test all touched apps), create README for the new app, document how to run/deploy.

evals.prompt: I need to flag something in a PR review: the code is committing a rayfin/.env file with workspace credentials. What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Immediate security flag: 'Credential leak detected — rayfin/.env should NEVER be committed. Add to .gitignore immediately. If already pushed, rotate credentials in Fabric workspace. Recommend: npm audit --fix, .gitignore pattern: rayfin/.env*'
